message:
fix(server): switch archive routes to filesystem-first project resolution and harden startup drift recovery

Archive and UI routes were tightly coupled to the live SQLite DB for
project resolution, causing 404s for projects that exist only in the
archive filesystem (e.g. after DB rebuilds or partial migrations).
This also meant startup probes could report healthy even when the
archive had drifted ahead of SQLite.

- Add resolve_mail_project_view() that tries DB first and falls back to
  archive on disk, returning (ProjectView, Option<i64>) where None ID
  indicates archive-only projects
- Add list_archive_projects(), resolve_archive_project(), and
  archive_project_human_key() for direct filesystem-based project
  metadata reading from project.json, bypassing DB entirely
- Refactor resolve_project_slug, render_archive_browser,
  render_archive_browser_file, render_archive_time_travel,
  render_archive_time_travel_snapshot, render_archive_guide, and
  render_api_project_agents to use archive lookups instead of DB queries
- Update render_file_reservations to use resolve_mail_project_view and
  return empty reservations for archive-only projects
- Rename template fields: reservations -> file_reservations,
  agent_name -> agent in ReservationView/FileReservationsCtx
- Convert mail_claims.html from layout-extending block to standalone partial
- Enhance startup probe to call compute_mailbox_verdict after integrity
  check, triggering attempt_probe_recovery on archive-ahead drift
- Update SnapshotDirGuard::new to use preferred_snapshot_temp_dir() with
  TMPDIR/TEMP/TMP env var validation instead of raw std::env::temp_dir()
- Add 6 new tests for archive fallback resolution, agent listing,
  file reservations, time travel snapshots, and dispatch routing
- Fix 2 existing tests to create real archive fixtures with
  with_process_env_overrides_for_test

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
